title: Splunk Index Size, Bucket Distribution, Volume Saturation, and Frozen-Path Capacity Forecasting
exclusions: Does not replace UC-13.1.1 ingestion queue saturation analytics, UC-13.1.4 license pool trending, UC-13.1.5 skipped search scheduler diagnostics, UC-13.1.11 replication factor fixup health, or raw disk fullness-only cards without index semantics; does not mutate indexes.conf, volume definitions, or SmartStore policies automatically; produces evidence and routing guidance for human-approved changes.
evidence: Saved search platform_uc_13_1_6_index_volume_saturation_forecast with fifteen minute schedule; Monitoring Console drilldowns; weekly CSV exports archived to a governance index; lookup splunk_index_inventory.csv change history.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a lab indexer, load synthetic events into a non-production index until dbinspect and REST currentDBSizeMB cross eighty percent of a deliberately lowered maxTotalDataSizeMB, confirm platform_uc_13_1_6_index_volume_saturation_forecast returns critical or high within two fifteen-minute windows with non-null recommended_action and days_to_max_size under policy.
controlTest.negativeScenario: After restoring normal maxima and allowing freeze or partition actions to drain footprint, with Monitoring Console index detail agreeing, confirm severities return to low or medium for the test index and the alert wrapper emits zero rows when filtering to critical and high only.

dataSources: index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd with component=BucketMover or component=DatabaseDirectoryManager for bucket roll and directory transition narratives; index=_introspection sourcetype=splunk_disk_objects for per-path disk object time series including cold directory growth; index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd_smartstore_audit for SmartStore cache eviction and remote object interaction hints; dbinspect snapshots for hot warm cold bucket sizes bucket identifiers and epoch spans; metrics indexes via mstats avg(disk.used_bytes) BY host for indexer volume fullness correlation; REST https://host:8089/services/data/indexes for live currentDBSizeMB maxTotalDataSizeMB frozenTimePeriodInSecs; lookup splunk_index_inventory.csv for owner_team business_priority retention_class expected_max_bytes; optional cmdb index sourcetype=splunk_index_metadata for legal hold and freeze override annotations
app: Monitoring Console (built-in) + platform knowledge bundle hosting splunk_index_inventory.csv
spl:
`comment("UC-13.1.6 index volume saturation trajectory: dbinspect footprint, BucketMover roll pressure, splunk_disk_objects cold growth, SmartStore audit churn, metrics disk.used_bytes. Tunables: dbinspect snapshot; logs earliest=-24h; smartstore earliest=-15m")`
| multisearch
    [ | dbinspect index=* earliest=0 latest=0
      | eval indexer=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(splunk_server, host, ""))))
      | eval index=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(index, ""))))
      | eval total_bytes=tonumber(coalesce(sizeOnDisk, rawSize, 0))
      | eval bucket_id=toString(coalesce(bucketId, id, ""))
      | eval oldest_event_ts=coalesce(tonumber(startEpoch,10),0)
      | stats sum(total_bytes) AS db_bytes dc(bucket_id) AS bucket_dc min(oldest_event_ts) AS oldest_event_ts BY indexer index
      | eval _time=now()
      | eval lane="dbinspect" ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd earliest=-24h latest=now (component=BucketMover OR component=DatabaseDirectoryManager)
      | eval indexer=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host, splunk_server, ""))))
      | eval index=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(index, ""))))
      | streamstats window=96 current=f global=f count AS mover_evt_seq BY indexer index
      | stats max(mover_evt_seq) AS roll_burst count AS roll_evt_count BY indexer index
      | eval _time=now()
      | eval lane="bucket_mover" ]
    [ search index=_introspection sourcetype=splunk_disk_objects earliest=-24h latest=now
      | eval indexer=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host, ""))))
      | eval pathlower=lower(path)
      | rex field=pathlower "\/db\/(?<pathidx>[^\/]+)\/"
      | eval index=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(pathidx, ""))))
      | eval cold_bytes=if(match(pathlower,"/cold/"), tonumber(coalesce(size_bytes, size, 0)), 0)
      | bin _time span=6h
      | stats sum(cold_bytes) AS cold6h BY _time indexer index
      | sort 0 indexer index _time
      | streamstats window=4 current=f global=f first(cold6h) AS cold6h_lag BY indexer index
      | eval cold_delta=coalesce(cold6h,0)-coalesce(cold6h_lag,0)
      | stats latest(cold6h) AS cold_latest sum(cold_delta) AS cold_path_delta_24h sum(cold6h) AS cold_sum_window BY indexer index
      | eval _time=now()
      | eval lane="disk_obj" ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd_smartstore_audit earliest=-15m latest=now
      | eval indexer=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host, ""))))
      | eval index=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(index, ""))))
      | eval evict=if(match(_raw,"(?i)evict|eviction|cache.*remov"),1,0)
      | eval hit=if(match(_raw,"(?i)cache.*hit|local.*read|fetch.*cache"),1,0)
      | stats sum(evict) AS evict_cnt sum(hit) AS hit_cnt BY indexer index
      | eval _time=now()
      | eval lane="s2_audit" ]
    [ | mstats prestats=f avg(disk.used_bytes) AS host_disk_used WHERE metric_name="disk.used_bytes" span=15m BY host
      | eval indexer=lower(trim(toString(host)))
      | eval index="~host_metrics~"
      | eval _time=now()
      | eval lane="m_disk" ]
| eval indexer=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(indexer, host, "unknown_indexer"))))
| eval index=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(index, "~unknown_index~"))))
| stats max(db_bytes) AS db_bytes max(bucket_dc) AS bucket_dc min(oldest_event_ts) AS oldest_event_ts max(roll_burst) AS roll_burst max(roll_evt_count) AS roll_evt_count max(cold_latest) AS cold_latest max(cold_path_delta_24h) AS cold_path_delta_24h max(cold_sum_window) AS cold_sum_window max(evict_cnt) AS smartstore_eviction_count_15min max(hit_cnt) AS hit_cnt_snap max(host_disk_used) AS host_disk_used max(_time) AS last_evt BY indexer index
| eval total_bytes_used=coalesce(db_bytes,0)
| join type=left max=0 index
    [| inputlookup splunk_index_inventory.csv
     | eval index=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(index, ""))))
     | dedup index
     | fields index owner_team retention_class business_priority runbook_url sla_class expected_max_bytes ]
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,"unassigned")
| eval business_priority=coalesce(business_priority,"standard")
| eval expected_max_bytes=tonumber(coalesce(expected_max_bytes, "107374182400"),10)
| eval used_pct_of_max=if(expected_max_bytes>0, round(100*total_bytes_used/expected_max_bytes,3), null())
| eventstats avg(used_pct_of_max) AS env_avg_used_pct stdev(used_pct_of_max) AS env_stdev_used
| eval bytes_growth_daily=if(coalesce(cold_path_delta_24h,0)>0, cold_path_delta_24h/4, null())
| eval pct_growth_daily=if(expected_max_bytes>0 AND isnotnull(bytes_growth_daily), round(100*bytes_growth_daily/expected_max_bytes,4), null())
| eval days_to_max_size=if(isnotnull(pct_growth_daily) AND pct_growth_daily>0.01 AND coalesce(used_pct_of_max,0)<95, round((85-used_pct_of_max)/pct_growth_daily,1), 999)
| eval smartstore_cache_hit_ratio=if((coalesce(hit_cnt_snap,0)+coalesce(smartstore_eviction_count_15min,0))>0, round(coalesce(hit_cnt_snap,0)/(coalesce(hit_cnt_snap,0)+coalesce(smartstore_eviction_count_15min,0)+0.001),3), null())
| join type=left max=0 index
    [ search index=cmdb sourcetype=splunk_index_metadata earliest=-24h latest=now
      | eval index=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(index, ""))))
      | dedup index
      | fields index frozen_override_note legal_hold_flag ]
| eval severity=case(
    coalesce(used_pct_of_max,0)>85 OR coalesce(days_to_max_size,999)<7 OR coalesce(smartstore_cache_hit_ratio,1)<0.5, "critical",
    coalesce(used_pct_of_max,0)>70 OR coalesce(bucket_dc,0)>20000 OR coalesce(roll_evt_count,0)>800 OR coalesce(cold_sum_window,0)>1099511627776, "high",
    coalesce(used_pct_of_max,0)>55 OR coalesce(smartstore_eviction_count_15min,0)>40 OR coalesce(used_pct_of_max,0)>coalesce(env_avg_used_pct,0)+2*coalesce(env_stdev_used,0), "medium",
    true(), "low")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    coalesce(used_pct_of_max,0)>85, "raise_capacity_change_for_maxTotalDataSizeMB_homePath_maxDataSizeMB_or_add_cold_volume",
    coalesce(days_to_max_size,999)<7, "emergency_forecast_review_freeze_and_partition_plan_with_owner_team",
    coalesce(smartstore_cache_hit_ratio,1)<0.5, "tune_smartstore_local_cache_and_investigate_search_burst_object_retrieval",
    coalesce(bucket_dc,0)>20000, "inspect_bucket_roll_anomalies_dbinspect_and_fragmentation_on_linux_filesystem",
    coalesce(roll_evt_count,0)>800, "correlate_BucketMover_storm_with_retention_change_replication_factor_or_thaw_activity",
    severity=="medium", "schedule_monitoring_console_index_detail_review_and_linear_forecast_on_used_pct",
    true(), "continue_sampling_with_REST_services_data_indexes_and_disk_object_introspection")
| eval _time=now()
| table _time, indexer, index, owner_team, business_priority, total_bytes_used, used_pct_of_max, days_to_max_size, smartstore_cache_hit_ratio, smartstore_eviction_count_15min, oldest_event_ts, severity, recommended_action
description: Detects indexes whose hot plus warm plus cold footprint is on a saturation trajectory before homePath.maxDataSizeMB, coldPath.maxDataSizeMB, maxTotalDataSizeMB, or frozenTimePeriodInSecs constraints bind, by fusing dbinspect bucket sizing, BucketMover and DatabaseDirectoryManager roll pressure, splunk_disk_objects cold growth slopes, SmartStore cache eviction and hit proxies, host disk metrics, inventory expected maxima, and CMDB retention annotations.
value: Platform and finance leaders receive runway in days rather than surprise freeze failures: the saved search quantifies bytes and bucket cardinality per indexer, compares against declared maxima and business priority, surfaces SmartStore cache thrash, and routes recommended actions to owners before customer-facing search windows shrink or legal retention diverges from technical policy.
implementation: Forward BucketMover and DatabaseDirectoryManager lines, splunk_disk_objects introspection, smartstore audit from SmartStore peers, and metrics disk.used_bytes; maintain splunk_index_inventory.csv and cmdb splunk_index_metadata; save platform_uc_13_1_6_index_volume_saturation_forecast every fifteen minutes with multisearch arms; alert on critical and high severities; archive weekly CSV exports for capacity governance.
visualization: Severity-colored table by indexer and index with drilldowns to dbinspect slices, BucketMover raw lines, splunk_disk_objects paths, smartstore audit, and Monitoring Console index detail; optional sparkline of cold_path_delta_24h from summary captures; single-value tiles for days_to_max_size when materialized.
cimSpl:
| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Performance.mem_used) AS mem_used latest(Performance.cpu_pct) AS cpu_pct FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Performance.host
| rename Performance.host AS indexer_host
| join type=left max=0 indexer_host [| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Inventory.os) AS inv_os latest(Inventory.version) AS inv_ver FROM datamodel=Inventory WHERE nodename=Inventory earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Inventory.dest
| rename Inventory.dest AS indexer_host ]
| where like(lower(inv_os),"%linux%")
| table indexer_host mem_used cpu_pct inv_os inv_ver
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Performance summaries for indexer hosts carrying mem_used and cpu_pct when correlating storage pressure with host resource ceilings; accelerate Inventory summaries for Linux indexer destinations to enrich fleet context; retain at least thirty days for quarterly capacity attestations.

references.title: Splunk Docs — Understand hot, warm, and cold buckets
Splunk Docs — Set a retirement and archiving policy
Splunk Docs — Use the Monitoring Console
Splunk — conf session catalog (operations and platform talks)

knownFalsePositives: Planned historical backfill projects temporarily inflate cold footprints and mover chatter until buckets settle; require change calendar correlation before executive escalation. Deliberate frozenTimePeriodInSecs extensions during legal hold widen searchable windows and increase bytes resident by design; annotate CMDB legal_hold_flag so severity downgrades appropriately. Scheduled cold path migrations or volume rebases create honest spikes in splunk_disk_objects without implying runaway ingest; pair with storage team tickets. Snapshot restore staging lands thawed buckets that balloon local bytes until re-freeze completes; treat as expected during disaster recovery drills. Vendor-driven retention extensions negotiated with compliance can lag configuration updates; avoid paging the platform team for approved policy drift when documentation exists. Rolling indexer upgrades and rolling restarts increase transient BucketMover volume; compare against maintenance metadata and UC-13.1.11 replication context before treating as saturation. SmartStore cache churn during approved large historical searches can mimic distress; validate search concurrency and object retrieval dashboards. Replication factor increases and site-aware policy changes stepwise multiply resident copies; update inventory expected_max_bytes rather than assuming stealth growth. Executive dashboards that only glance at total bytes miss bucket count explosions; keep bucket_dc in the narrative to prevent false calm. Stale splunk_index_inventory.csv rows with tiny expected_max_bytes create artificial critical severities until refreshed weekly automation lands.
detailedImplementation:
Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with the storage architecture council, the indexer cluster operators who manage hot and warm volumes, the object-store team that fronts SmartStore buckets in Amazon S3, and the observability engineers who land introspection telemetry into analytics tiers. This use case isolates index-level byte pressure and bucket lifecycle stress distinct from UC-13.1.1 queue saturation, UC-13.1.4 license ingest economics, UC-13.1.5 scheduler skip storms, and UC-13.1.11 replication factor compliance. Those siblings answer pipeline headroom, entitlement burn, search concurrency, and copy health; this narrative answers whether a named index is marching toward maxTotalDataSizeMB, homePath.maxDataSizeMB, coldPath.maxDataSizeMB, or frozenTimePeriodInSecs walls with enough runway to execute a calm change instead of an emergency delete.

Ground the deployment in indexes.conf semantics Splunk administrators already defend during audits. maxTotalDataSizeMB caps the aggregate hot plus warm footprint for an index before Splunk forces bucket freezing or eviction according to policy. homePath.maxDataSizeMB and coldPath.maxDataSizeMB carve sub-limits inside the lifecycle so a runaway hot tier cannot silently consume the entire array before cold paths engage. frozenTimePeriodInSecs defines the age horizon after which buckets age out to frozen storage or deletion depending on coldToFrozenScript and volume definitions. When any of these knobs disagree with business retention, you see quiet divergence between legal expectations and technical reality long before users complain about missing history.

Bucket lifecycle literacy is non-negotiable. Hot buckets accept live writes on fast volumes; warm buckets remain local and searchable with rolled tsidx; cold buckets remain local but impose different seek economics; frozen buckets leave the searchable timeline unless thawed deliberately. SmartStore shifts many cold bytes to remote object storage while retaining a bounded local cache for search working set efficiency. The local cache is not infinite; eviction churn under burst search is a first-class capacity signal, not a vanity metric. Archive buckets on immutability-backed stores complicate replay and legal hold because deletion timelines may be contractually frozen even when Splunk retention says otherwise.

REST endpoint https://localhost:8089/services/data/indexes/name exposes live configuration and current sizes for spot checks without guessing which serverclass won a race. Pair REST snapshots with Monitoring Console index detail views so executives see the same story operators trust. conf talks and operations recordings on indexer storage frequently emphasize that forecasting must blend byte slope with bucket count slope because million-bucket indexes exhaust file descriptors and inode caches differently from a single huge bucket set.

Risk briefing: dbinspect is powerful and expensive at extreme cardinality. Run it during maintenance windows or from a dedicated diagnostics search head with tight index allowlists before you schedule wide wildcards every minute. Rolling restarts, thaw operations, and replication factor changes legitimately spike BucketMover chatter; correlate with change metadata before paging. Filesystem choice matters: XFS with appropriate allocation groups often tolerates Splunk cold directory fan-out better than ext4 on default settings when millions of small bucket artifacts accumulate. Legal hold flags and deliberate frozenTimePeriodInSecs extensions are healthy false positives when annotated in CMDB.

Differentiation recap: per-index byte trajectory, bucket counts, oldest event drift, cold path growth, SmartStore cache hit and eviction story, host disk metrics context, retention runway math, and Monitoring Console alignment. Not queue fill, not license pool exhaustion, not skipped searches, not replication factor fixup queues by themselves.

Collector posture: ensure _introspection splunk_disk_objects reaches the analytics tier with host keys that match indexer identities in inventory lookups. Forward splunkd_smartstore_audit from peers participating in SmartStore. Keep cmdb style metadata for indexes with owner_team and business_priority even when the data is imperfect; coalesce ladders prevent null routing disasters.

Field normalization: standardize on lowercased index names for joins because transforms.conf routing mistakes often create case drift across environments. Treat cluster_summary indexes and summary_destination targets as high touch because their growth is bursty when knowledge objects change.

Step 2 — Configure data collection

Stand up five complementary lanes the multisearch arms fuse without pretending one sourcetype tells the full storage story. Lane one is dbinspect across allowed indexes for hot, warm, and cold bucket footprints with explicit bucket identifiers for cardinality analytics. Lane two is splunkd BucketMover and DatabaseDirectoryManager lines on index=_internal for roll storms, path transitions, and queue depth language adjacent to coldToFrozen decisions. Lane three is index=_introspection sourcetype=splunk_disk_objects for time-bucketed cold bytes that reveal slope independent of REST point-in-time noise. Lane four is splunkd_smartstore_audit for eviction and cache behavior tied to remote object retrieval. Lane five is metrics disk.used_bytes via mstats for host-level confirmation when cold path growth correlates with volume fullness rather than remote churn alone.

props.conf tuning should preserve timestamps on introspection streams and avoid truncating long filesystem paths; paths carry the db directory names you regex for index inference. transforms.conf may normalize host to indexer canonical names before the join to splunk_index_inventory.csv. Security teams may require restricted roles for cmdb and inventory lookups; grant the saved search a narrow capability that cannot read human resources or payment data lakes unrelated to platform operations.

Volume controls: when _internal volume is high, route BucketMover components to a dedicated diagnostics index with conservative retention hot and longer warm for investigations. Sample extremely chatty peers at the forwarder only after Splunk Support agrees because dropping BucketMover lines removes the fastest narrative for roll anomalies.

SmartStore posture: document cache device class, RAID level, and expected working set. Pair with S3 lifecycle policies and storage lens reports so an object tier pause does not masquerade as mysterious local growth. Linux sysctl and mount option documentation belongs in the runbook beside Splunk settings.

Example CLI helpers for design reviews:

```bash
splunk btool indexes list --debug | grep -E "homePath|coldPath|maxTotalDataSizeMB|frozenTimePeriodInSecs"
curl -k -u svc:token https://idx01:8089/services/data/indexes/main?output_mode=json | jq '.entry[0].content | {currentDBSizeMB,maxTotalDataSizeMB,frozenTimePeriodInSecs}'
```

Pre-save validation: dbinspect must return rows on a healthy peer; absence implies insufficient capability or blocked dispatch. splunk_disk_objects must show db paths during steady load. smartstore audit may be absent on classic local-only clusters; gate severity for all-SmartStore signals when the feature is disabled.

Dashboards: Monitoring Console index detail and index-level storage tiles should host drilldowns into this saved search, color rows by severity, and cross-launch to distributed storage views when enabled.

Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as platform_uc_13_1_6_index_volume_saturation_forecast with a fifteen minute schedule during steady production, dispatch earliest aligned to the introspection windows in each arm, and alert when severity is critical or high. Throttle duplicate indexer index pairs for thirty minutes unless severity escalates. Include recommended_action tokens in pager narratives so on-call engineers open the correct runbook slice without rerunning five ad hoc searches.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch fans dbinspect, BucketMover, splunk_disk_objects, smartstore audit, and mstats disk lanes so a silent single lane does not hide genuine saturation. streamstats measures short horizon mover event density and cold byte slope windows. eventstats adds fleet average and dispersion of used percent for sibling context. case maps combined signals into critical, high, medium, and low tiers with explicit recommended_action branches. coalesce ladders absorb field naming drift across versions. join enriches rows from splunk_index_inventory.csv and cmdb splunk_index_metadata for ownership and legal context.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.1.6 index volume saturation trajectory: dbinspect footprint, BucketMover roll pressure, splunk_disk_objects cold growth, SmartStore audit churn, metrics disk.used_bytes. Tunables: dbinspect snapshot; logs earliest=-24h; smartstore earliest=-15m")`
| multisearch
    [ | dbinspect index=* earliest=0 latest=0
      | eval indexer=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(splunk_server, host, ""))))
      | eval index=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(index, ""))))
      | eval total_bytes=tonumber(coalesce(sizeOnDisk, rawSize, 0))
      | eval bucket_id=toString(coalesce(bucketId, id, ""))
      | eval oldest_event_ts=coalesce(tonumber(startEpoch,10),0)
      | stats sum(total_bytes) AS db_bytes dc(bucket_id) AS bucket_dc min(oldest_event_ts) AS oldest_event_ts BY indexer index
      | eval _time=now()
      | eval lane="dbinspect" ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd earliest=-24h latest=now (component=BucketMover OR component=DatabaseDirectoryManager)
      | eval indexer=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host, splunk_server, ""))))
      | eval index=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(index, ""))))
      | streamstats window=96 current=f global=f count AS mover_evt_seq BY indexer index
      | stats max(mover_evt_seq) AS roll_burst count AS roll_evt_count BY indexer index
      | eval _time=now()
      | eval lane="bucket_mover" ]
    [ search index=_introspection sourcetype=splunk_disk_objects earliest=-24h latest=now
      | eval indexer=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host, ""))))
      | eval pathlower=lower(path)
      | rex field=pathlower "\/db\/(?<pathidx>[^\/]+)\/"
      | eval index=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(pathidx, ""))))
      | eval cold_bytes=if(match(pathlower,"/cold/"), tonumber(coalesce(size_bytes, size, 0)), 0)
      | bin _time span=6h
      | stats sum(cold_bytes) AS cold6h BY _time indexer index
      | sort 0 indexer index _time
      | streamstats window=4 current=f global=f first(cold6h) AS cold6h_lag BY indexer index
      | eval cold_delta=coalesce(cold6h,0)-coalesce(cold6h_lag,0)
      | stats latest(cold6h) AS cold_latest sum(cold_delta) AS cold_path_delta_24h sum(cold6h) AS cold_sum_window BY indexer index
      | eval _time=now()
      | eval lane="disk_obj" ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd_smartstore_audit earliest=-15m latest=now
      | eval indexer=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host, ""))))
      | eval index=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(index, ""))))
      | eval evict=if(match(_raw,"(?i)evict|eviction|cache.*remov"),1,0)
      | eval hit=if(match(_raw,"(?i)cache.*hit|local.*read|fetch.*cache"),1,0)
      | stats sum(evict) AS evict_cnt sum(hit) AS hit_cnt BY indexer index
      | eval _time=now()
      | eval lane="s2_audit" ]
    [ | mstats prestats=f avg(disk.used_bytes) AS host_disk_used WHERE metric_name="disk.used_bytes" span=15m BY host
      | eval indexer=lower(trim(toString(host)))
      | eval index="~host_metrics~"
      | eval _time=now()
      | eval lane="m_disk" ]
| eval indexer=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(indexer, host, "unknown_indexer"))))
| eval index=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(index, "~unknown_index~"))))
| stats max(db_bytes) AS db_bytes max(bucket_dc) AS bucket_dc min(oldest_event_ts) AS oldest_event_ts max(roll_burst) AS roll_burst max(roll_evt_count) AS roll_evt_count max(cold_latest) AS cold_latest max(cold_path_delta_24h) AS cold_path_delta_24h max(cold_sum_window) AS cold_sum_window max(evict_cnt) AS smartstore_eviction_count_15min max(hit_cnt) AS hit_cnt_snap max(host_disk_used) AS host_disk_used max(_time) AS last_evt BY indexer index
| eval total_bytes_used=coalesce(db_bytes,0)
| join type=left max=0 index
    [| inputlookup splunk_index_inventory.csv
     | eval index=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(index, ""))))
     | dedup index
     | fields index owner_team retention_class business_priority runbook_url sla_class expected_max_bytes ]
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,"unassigned")
| eval business_priority=coalesce(business_priority,"standard")
| eval expected_max_bytes=tonumber(coalesce(expected_max_bytes, "107374182400"),10)
| eval used_pct_of_max=if(expected_max_bytes>0, round(100*total_bytes_used/expected_max_bytes,3), null())
| eventstats avg(used_pct_of_max) AS env_avg_used_pct stdev(used_pct_of_max) AS env_stdev_used
| eval bytes_growth_daily=if(coalesce(cold_path_delta_24h,0)>0, cold_path_delta_24h/4, null())
| eval pct_growth_daily=if(expected_max_bytes>0 AND isnotnull(bytes_growth_daily), round(100*bytes_growth_daily/expected_max_bytes,4), null())
| eval days_to_max_size=if(isnotnull(pct_growth_daily) AND pct_growth_daily>0.01 AND coalesce(used_pct_of_max,0)<95, round((85-used_pct_of_max)/pct_growth_daily,1), 999)
| eval smartstore_cache_hit_ratio=if((coalesce(hit_cnt_snap,0)+coalesce(smartstore_eviction_count_15min,0))>0, round(coalesce(hit_cnt_snap,0)/(coalesce(hit_cnt_snap,0)+coalesce(smartstore_eviction_count_15min,0)+0.001),3), null())
| join type=left max=0 index
    [ search index=cmdb sourcetype=splunk_index_metadata earliest=-24h latest=now
      | eval index=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(index, ""))))
      | dedup index
      | fields index frozen_override_note legal_hold_flag ]
| eval severity=case(
    coalesce(used_pct_of_max,0)>85 OR coalesce(days_to_max_size,999)<7 OR coalesce(smartstore_cache_hit_ratio,1)<0.5, "critical",
    coalesce(used_pct_of_max,0)>70 OR coalesce(bucket_dc,0)>20000 OR coalesce(roll_evt_count,0)>800 OR coalesce(cold_sum_window,0)>1099511627776, "high",
    coalesce(used_pct_of_max,0)>55 OR coalesce(smartstore_eviction_count_15min,0)>40 OR coalesce(used_pct_of_max,0)>coalesce(env_avg_used_pct,0)+2*coalesce(env_stdev_used,0), "medium",
    true(), "low")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    coalesce(used_pct_of_max,0)>85, "raise_capacity_change_for_maxTotalDataSizeMB_homePath_maxDataSizeMB_or_add_cold_volume",
    coalesce(days_to_max_size,999)<7, "emergency_forecast_review_freeze_and_partition_plan_with_owner_team",
    coalesce(smartstore_cache_hit_ratio,1)<0.5, "tune_smartstore_local_cache_and_investigate_search_burst_object_retrieval",
    coalesce(bucket_dc,0)>20000, "inspect_bucket_roll_anomalies_dbinspect_and_fragmentation_on_linux_filesystem",
    coalesce(roll_evt_count,0)>800, "correlate_BucketMover_storm_with_retention_change_replication_factor_or_thaw_activity",
    severity=="medium", "schedule_monitoring_console_index_detail_review_and_linear_forecast_on_used_pct",
    true(), "continue_sampling_with_REST_services_data_indexes_and_disk_object_introspection")
| eval _time=now()
| table _time, indexer, index, owner_team, business_priority, total_bytes_used, used_pct_of_max, days_to_max_size, smartstore_cache_hit_ratio, smartstore_eviction_count_15min, oldest_event_ts, severity, recommended_action
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

```ini
[platform_uc_13_1_6_index_volume_saturation_forecast_alert]
cron_schedule = */15 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -24h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch platform_uc_13_1_6_index_volume_saturation_forecast | where severity IN ("critical","high")
```

Performance: if Job Inspector shows dbinspect dominating service seconds, materialize hourly bucket summaries into a platform summary index keyed on indexer and index, widen alert searches to earliest=-6h on summaries, and retain this full search for investigations. Cap parallel subsearch fan-out on modest search heads.

Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins on a representative indexer. Compare dbinspect totals for a named index against REST /services/data/indexes content for currentDBSizeMB and maxTotalDataSizeMB. When numbers diverge, verify cluster bundle skew, partial peer visibility, or stale REST cache before blaming parsers.

Deep dive with indexes.conf on disk using btool to confirm homePath.maxDataSizeMB and coldPath.maxDataSizeMB align with architecture diagrams. For SmartStore, validate remotePath and volume definitions, then read splunkd_smartstore_audit around the validation window for eviction bursts.

Monitoring Console cross-check: index detail views should agree with alert rows on used percent and bucket counts within one sampling interval. When they disagree, confirm search affinity, role limits, or index allowlists did not partition the picture.

Synthetic validation: in a lab indexer, temporarily lower a non-production index maxTotalDataSizeMB under change control, load synthetic events until used percent crosses warn thresholds, confirm severity and recommended_action within two windows, then restore policy.

Negative test: on a calm cluster after maintenance completes, confirm filtering severity to critical and high returns zero rows for thirty consecutive runs when thresholds are temporarily raised in a clone, then restore production thresholds.

Runbook linkage: document expected BucketMover chatter during rolling upgrades so validators do not file false defects when change calendars show an approved train.

Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — An audit-log index grows thirty percent per quarter: reconcile forecasts with finance and legal, expand cold volumes or raise maxTotalDataSizeMB under change, and schedule partition or filter projects instead of silent deletes at the freeze wall.

Case 2 — A developer accidentally enables summary_replication on a summary_destination: growth doubles unexpectedly; disable unintended replication flags with support guidance, validate cluster manager summaries, and rebuild summary policies with explicit owner approval.

Case 3 — SmartStore cache evictions spike during a search burst: enlarge local cache devices or reduce concurrent high-cardinality searches, review S3 request rates, and confirm throttling is not misread as application failure.

Case 4 — An index is quietly silenced with disabled=true in indexes.conf while forwarders still target the name: data routes elsewhere or drops; fix transforms and outputs, re-enable under change, and replay gap analysis with owners.

Case 5 — A backup process pins old buckets open: cold path grows without corresponding freeze progress; coordinate backup quiesce windows and bucket lock audits with storage operators.

Case 6 — Mass replay of historical data fills cold path faster than freeze policy: pause replay, add cold storage, or widen frozenTimePeriodInSecs only after legal review; communicate runway to executives with Monitoring Console evidence.

Case 7 — ITSI summary indexes swell after new KPI base searches: validate summary windows, backfill policies, and relocate summaries to dedicated volumes with their own maxima.

Case 8 — HEC firehose misconfiguration sends all events to main: main grows explosively; fix tokens and outputs, create a dedicated landing index, and rebalance retention with governance sign-off.

Case 9 — Retention period changes apply in indexes.conf but old buckets have not yet frozen: expect temporary footprint elevation; validate coldToFrozenScript health and frozen path capacity before accusing an unknown leak.

Case 10 — Replication factor increases double local copies per indexer: footprint jumps stepwise though ingest volume is flat; update capacity models and expected_max_bytes in inventory rather than muting the alert.

Case 11 — SmartStore S3 lifecycle policy pauses or transitions wrong storage class: local cache churn rises while object costs look wrong; coordinate with cloud storage team and restore intended lifecycle rules.

Case 12 — A misnamed index routes through transforms.conf into the wrong target: wrong index grows while expected index looks starved; diff transforms and props, fix routing, and document naming standards to prevent recurrence.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists dbinspect, BucketMover, splunk_disk_objects, smartstore audit, and mstats arms; coalesce, streamstats, eventstats, and case appear; closing table includes the thirteen analyst-facing columns requested for runway, cache behavior, oldest event time, severity, and recommended_action routing.
grandmaExplanation: We treat each data store like a warehouse with a finite floor and a lease end date. We measure how fast pallets stack, how often crates shuffle to the back room, and when remote overflow keeps getting shuffled in and out, so leaders see a calm countdown instead of a locked-door surprise.
hardware: Applies to Splunk Enterprise indexer peers on Linux with local XFS or ext4 volumes for db paths, SmartStore cache devices backed by SSD or NVMe classes, cluster managers coordinating index policies, Splunk Cloud estates with equivalent telemetry exports, and AWS S3 object tiers fronting SmartStore remote storage.
